title: Starting podman (5.7.0-rc2) Machine on Mac OS Error: krunkit was terminated by signal: abort trap
body:
### Issue Description

Cannot start the podman machine with libkrun provider on Mac OS using podman 5.7.0-rc2 bits.

Getting:
```
Machine init complete
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: krunkit was terminated by signal: abort trap
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman from 5.7.0-rc2 pkg installer
2. export CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER='libkrun'
3. podman machine init --now


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:
```
CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER='libkrun' podman machine init --now                  
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.7 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5f531b748881 done   | 
Copying config 44136fa355 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
5f531b748881e9f13ffc699fdec01738253a465bba45d8a9cfb36d84bebdbe0e
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: done  
Machine init complete
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: krunkit was terminated by signal: abort trap
```

```
krunkit -v
dyld[17236]: Library not loaded: /opt/homebrew/opt/libkrun-efi/lib/libkrun-efi.1.dylib
  Referenced from: <9B9436E4-A485-380C-88B1-BA4E04463337> /opt/podman/bin/krunkit
  Reason: tried: '/opt/homebrew/opt/libkrun-efi/lib/libkrun-efi.1.dylib' (no such file), '/System/Volumes/Preboot/Cryptexes/OS/opt/homebrew/opt/libkrun-efi/lib/libkrun-efi.1.dylib' (no such file), '/opt/homebrew/opt/libkrun-efi/lib/libkrun-efi.1.dylib' (no such file)
zsh: abort      krunkit -v
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected:
Machine is started successfully. 

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

podman -v     
podman version 5.7.0-rc2
podmanqe@dev-platform-mac7 bin % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
buildOrigin: pkginstaller
provider: libkrun
version: 5.7.0-rc2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

sw_vers:
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		14.8
BuildVersion:		23J21

(but saw the same on other os versions)

### Additional information

Works well with 5.6.2. Also, using apple HV seems to work.

comments.body:
@slp @jakecorrenti PTAL

I assume the bundled krunkit version from https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27393 is broken then?
I can reproduce. Still need to determine cause.

@slp I'm noticing that the homebrew formulae for krunkit and libkrun are both out of date. Could that be a factor here?

Building krunkit from source w/ what's currently in brew works fine. Of course what's in brew for krunkit and libkrun is also working. I'm running into some build system issues to get libkrunfw-efi built, but I'm testing that as well to see if there's anything wrong on that front

The culprit is that `krunkit` has started linking against `libkrun-efi.1.dylib` instead of `libkrun-efi.dylib`. This broke `create_release_podman.sh` and caused the binary to slip a reference to `/opt/homebrew/lib...`. This didn't fail in the test system, because the library was present in the Homebrew prefix, but fails systems that don't have the library there.

I've created an expedited `1.1.1` release to fix the bundle package in podman. I've also [added a test](https://github.com/containers/krunkit/blob/db86ebcd3ad0f25cce9557ef0be02a922ce1ec49/create_release_podman.sh#L44) in `create_release_podman.sh` to ensure next time we can detect this issue before publishing the release.
@odockal Thank you for the early report! 👏
